.Optical Model Potential.
The Twice-Scattered Protons are Detected in
Ilford C2 Emulsions Placed in the Second Scattering
Chamber.
/by Authors/.The Polarized Beam is Achieved by
Irradiating a Gaseous Hydrogen Target with 25-MeV Alpha
Particles and Extracting the Protons Which are
Deflected Through 130 Degree in the C.M. System,i.e.,
Those Protons Which Recoil at 25 Degree to the Primary
Beam Direction.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
The Tabulated Uncertainties Represent a Quadratic
Combination of the Various Errors.
The Dominant Contribution to Polarization Uncertainty
Resides in the Statistical Ubcertainty.
The Major Systematic Error was Introduced by
Ever-Present Possibility of Unresolved Inelastic
Scattering Corresponding to Low-Lying Levels in the
Residual Nucleus.
